data.title: Assessing Lonza Group’s Valuation As FDA AI Collaboration Puts Its Drug Development Capabilities In Focus
data.description:
Lonza Group (SWX:LONN) has moved into focus after joining a funded research collaboration with Simulations Plus and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to build predictive frameworks for amorphous solid dispersion drug products.

See our latest analysis for Lonza Group.

Despite the new FDA-backed collaboration and recent conference appearances, Lonza Group’s 1-day share price return of 1.83% comes against a weaker backdrop. The 90-day share price return of 12.5% and the 1-year total shareholder return of 15.26% suggest momentum has been fading rather than building.

If this kind of pharma and biotech exposure interests you, it could be worth broadening your search using a focused screener to spot other opportunities in healthcare related AI, including 124 healthcare AI stocks

So with Lonza Group’s recent share price softness and ongoing FDA backed research work in AI enabled drug development, are investors looking at an underappreciated pharma services leader, or is the current valuation already reflecting future growth?

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 37.5x: Is it justified?

Lonza Group closed at CHF488.6, and the current P/E of 37.5x sits at the higher end of what investors are paying for life sciences earnings today.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share and gives a quick read on how much the market is paying for current profits. For a contract development and manufacturing organisation serving pharma and biotech clients across biologics, small molecules and specialized modalities, earnings quality and growth expectations often matter more than headline revenue size.

On one hand, Lonza Group looks expensive compared to an estimated fair P/E of 32.5x and the global life sciences industry average of 34.2x. That suggests the share price embeds a richer earnings multiple than both a model based fair ratio and the wider peer set. This is a level the market could move towards if sentiment cools. On the other hand, the P/E of 37.5x is slightly below the peer average of 39.6x in its comparison group, which hints that investors are not paying the absolute top tier multiple for a company with 51.2% earnings growth over the past year and higher current profit margins than last year.

However, the 1 year total shareholder return of 15.26%, alongside recent 7 day and 90 day share price declines, suggests sentiment could sour further if growth stalls or margins tighten.

Another View: Cash Flows Point to a Different Story

While the P/E of 37.5x suggests Lonza Group is only slightly rich compared with peers, the SWS DCF model presents a tougher picture. On this view, the shares at CHF488.6 sit well above an estimated future cash flow value of CHF230.15, which indicates meaningful valuation risk if expectations cool.

Approach 1: Medpace Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to today’s value using a required rate of return. It aims to answer what those future cash flows are worth in today’s dollars.

For Medpace Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $699.0 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2030, where free cash flow is projected at $852.5 million, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates further years using its own assumptions.

Discounting these projected cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $598.59 per share. Compared with the recent share price around $421, the DCF output implies the shares trade at a 29.6% discount to this estimate. This indicates that Medpace appears undervalued on this specific cash flow model.

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. Investors usually accept a higher P/E when they expect stronger growth or see lower risk, and look for a lower P/E when growth expectations are more modest or risks feel higher.

Medpace Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 26.14x. That sits below the Life Sciences industry average P/E of 34.68x and also below the peer group average of 44.63x. On the surface, that suggests the market is paying less for Medpace’s earnings than for many similar companies.
